title: streptanthus glandulosus subsp. josephinensis
intro: Streptanthus glandulosus subsp. josephinensis, also known as Joseph's jewel-flower, is a rare and endangered plant species native to California in the United States. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Joseph's jewel-flower include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and support immune function. It can be prepared as a tea or tincture through herbal preparations, but more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety as a medicinal plant.
